_comment: Lifecycle flags for companies that should be flagged in private_companies (acquired / public / defunct). These rows still get loaded into private_companies (so historical N-PORT exposure is queryable) but are filtered out of the current-period rollup via the materialized view's WHERE clause (NOT is_sanctioned AND NOT is_acquired AND NOT is_public). See PLAN_NPORT_HOLDINGS.md §3.2 (Recursion, Ginkgo callouts) and §4.1 (lifecycle_status field).

_field_meanings.is_acquired: true → exit was an M&A event
_field_meanings.acquired_date: ISO date when N-PORT rows began disappearing
_field_meanings.is_public: true → exit was an IPO; we exclude from the private rollup
_field_meanings.ipo_date: ISO date of first trade (only when is_public=true)

entries.note: Acquired by Google for $32B; close completed Q1 2025. Plan §6.7 explicitly calls this out — has 0 N-PORT rows now.
Trades on NASDAQ as CFLT. Exclude from private rollup.
Trades on NASDAQ as RIVN. Exclude from private rollup.
Trades on NASDAQ as RXRX. Mentioned by name in PLAN §3.1 as a false-positive substring match against the private 'Recursion' SPV namespace.
Trades on NYSE as DNA via SPAC merger. Exclude from private rollup.
IPO pending — keep in private rollup until first trade. Flagged here for visibility because filings indicate imminent listing.
Appears in Wikipedia 'exited unicorns' table — flag so any N-PORT residual rows are excluded from private rollup.
Still private as of 2026 Q1. Listed here only to confirm explicit private status (no override).
